title: Skeptic Triage
description: The ADVERSE-ONLY output of the skeptic pass -- both the run-scoped per-batch fragment (triage_{index}.json) and the single merged artifact (default {durable_root}/skeptic_triage.json, produced by skeptic_ready.py --merge-fragments). SAFETY INVARIANT (round-4 blocker 2, contract-following agent): `verdict` is drawn ONLY from {adverse, propose_split, propose_rescope, insufficient_window} -- there is DELIBERATELY no verdict, field, or flag able to express a confirmation ('this identity is correct'), and additionalProperties:false at every level rejects a smuggled confirmation-shaped field (e.g. confirmed_ok) LOUD. NO freeze/merge reader opens this file; every confirmation still flows through the unchanged human/codex canon_adjudications.json / canon_senses.json paths. skeptic_ready.py re-authenticates every cited evidence record via evidence_verify machinery before accept, and enforces the verdict-specific procedural requirements this schema documents but leaves to code (mirroring canon_senses.py::load_senses): propose_split REQUIRES referents (>=2, each with byte-verified evidence); adverse/propose_rescope REQUIRE a single byte-verified contradicting evidence citation; insufficient_window requires neither. Any citation that will not byte-verify (incl. any embedded-verse-node window) is coerced to insufficient_window (fail-closed).

records.description: One record per examined assignment (a fragment carries its batch's records; the merged file carries all). An empty array is the schema-valid 'no adverse findings' state.

assignment_id.description: Joins back to the assignment manifest (coverage check).

verdict.description: ADVERSE-ONLY. No confirmation value exists by construction.

rationale.description: Human-readable reason (the contradicting observation / why the window was insufficient).

evidence.description: The single byte-verified contradicting citation. Present for adverse / propose_rescope (procedurally required by skeptic_ready.py); absent for insufficient_window.

referents.description: Distinct referents for a proposed homonym split. Present ONLY for propose_split, where skeptic_ready.py procedurally requires it; minItems:2 enforces the Phase-1 >=2-senses split shape whenever the property is used.

disambiguator.description: Short gloss distinguishing this referent from its siblings.

evidence_coverage.description: How many cited evidence records byte-verified (verified <= cited). Rendered by skeptic_report.py; partial coverage shown as partial.

evidence.description: The SAME minimal-verifiable evidence shape canon-senses.schema.json uses (block-anchored, byte-authenticated). occ_index emits context_sha256; skeptic_ready.py remaps it to `sha256` before calling verify_evidence.
